title: Innovative Intense Malaysian Tofu Bake
description: A resinous, aerated, meal-prep Malaysian dish featuring silken tofu, this bake is sugar-free-friendly and perfect for a weeknight dinner. It combines the flavors of Malaysia with the ease of a casserole.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender or food processor, combine coconut milk, malaysian curry powder, salt, and black pepper, and blend until smooth.
In a large bowl, toss the cubed tofu with the blended curry mixture until the tofu is well coated.
In a large baking dish, arrange the coated tofu in a single layer.
Drizzle the olive oil over the tofu, then sprinkle the chopped scallions and bruised lemongrass over the top.
Cover the baking dish with aluminum foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 15 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned.
Remove the baking dish from the oven and stir in the kaffir lime leaves.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes before serving.
